column4: Train Strikes and Kills Gerardo Perez in Shafter, Authorities Say - Accident Injury News
column7:
Train Strikes and Kills Gerardo Perez in Shafter, Authorities Say

SHAFTER, CA — January 17, 2026 — A 40-year-old man was killed Saturday morning after being struck by a train in Shafter, according to authorities. The Kern County Coroner’s Office identified the victim as Gerardo Becerra Perez, a resident of Shafter. Law enforcement officials said Perez was hit by a train near the intersection of South Beech Avenue and East Los Angeles Avenue. Officers responded to the scene at approximately 9:34 a.m., where Perez was pronounced dead. The circumstances leading up to the collision were not immediately released, and the incident remains under investigation.



 Each year, hundreds of lives are lost in train-related incidents across the United States, many occurring at crossings with limited warning signs, faulty signals, or inadequate fencing. According to the National Safety Council, railroad fatalities reached 954 in 2024, reflecting a 1% decrease from the revised 2023 total of 967. Candice Bond, a seasoned train accident attorney, emphasized the importance of conducting an independent investigation promptly following a fatal crash to help preserve critical evidence for a victim’s family. “An experienced attorney can help shield grieving families from large railroad companies, which often deploy their own investigative teams and have extensive resources to defend against claims,” said Bond, Managing Partner of Bond Legal. “Acting quickly is crucial in these cases to ensure that all evidence is properly examined,” Bond added. 

 Pending the outcome of the investigation, surviving family members of Gerardo Becerra Perez may have the right to pursue a wrongful death claim. In such cases, families may seek compensation for loss of companionship, emotional distress, and the pain and suffering caused by the sudden loss of a loved one. “The emotional toll following the unexpected death of a family member can be devastating,” said Stefano Formica, Of Counsel with Bond Legal. “A wrongful death attorney can closely monitor the investigation while working to protect the legal rights and interests of the victim’s family,” Formica said.

column4: Train Collides with SUV in Hesperia; Driver Escapes Unharmed - vvng.com
column7:
HESPERIA, Calif. (VVNG.com) — A train struck an SUV Sunday evening in Hesperia, but the driver was able to escape moments before impact.
The incident was reported at approximately 6:13 p.m. on January 25, 2026, near the intersection of W. Santa Fe Avenue and Deodar Street.
A white SUV became disabled on the tracks and was struck by a BNSF freight train hauling tanker cars.
Emergency crews responded to the scene and determined that no injuries occurred as a result of the crash.
According to preliminary information, the driver of the SUV managed to exit the vehicle before it was hit.
It is believed the driver may have fallen asleep prior to the collision. Drugs or alcohol are not suspected to be factors in the incident.
Train traffic was temporarily halted while crews worked to remove the damaged vehicle from the tracks.
The investigation is being handled by BNSF Police, and no further details have been released.

column4: Ontario, CA – Fatal Train Crash at E Airport Dr & N Archibald Ave - localaccidentreports.com
column7:
Ontario, CA (January 25, 2026) – A deadly incident occurred late Saturday morning involving a train and a pedestrian near the intersection of East Airport Drive and North Archibald Avenue in the city of Ontario. Authorities responded to the scene shortly after 11:25 a.m. on January 24 following reports of a person struck on the tracks.

According to dispatch records, a Union Pacific train was involved in the fatal collision, which occurred within the jurisdiction of the California Highway Patrol’s Inland Division. The pedestrian, identified only as a trespasser at this time, was pronounced deceased at the scene. Responding officers requested the San Bernardino County Coroner’s Office shortly after confirming the fatality.

Officials have not released the identity of the victim, and it remains unclear how the individual came to be on the tracks. There is currently no estimated time for coroner arrival, and the area was temporarily held for investigation and scene management.

This tragic event remains under active investigation. Union Pacific and local law enforcement are working to determine the circumstances that led to the fatal encounter.

Our hearts go out to the family and loved ones of the individual who lost their life in this tragic incident.

column4: Jamaal Scott dies in Ontario train accident, coroner says - The Legal Advocate
column7:
Pedestrian fatally hit by train near Archibald Avenue and Airport Drive
ONTARIO, CA, (January 25, 2026) - A 43-year-old man tragically died Sunday morning in a train vs. pedestrian accident in Ontario, according to a coroner's press release from the San Bernardino County Sheriff's Department.
Jamaal Scott died Sunday morning in a train vs. pedestrian accident near Archibald Avenue and Airport Drive in Ontario.
The report says Jamaal Lionel Scott was hit by a train near Archibald Avenue and Airport Drive at approximately 10:49 a.m.
Sadly, Scott was declared dead at the scene.
Additional details were not initially released.
The Ontario Police Department is investigating.
FATAL PEDESTRIAN TRAIN ACCIDENT INVESTIGATION
Train accidents such as this can occur for several reasons, including train personnel error, train track failure, a mechanical error or malfunction, a signalman error, and reckless actions. During the probe, investigators will also examine if the crash site was made safe for the pedestrian crossing.
According to the National Transportation Safety Bureau (NTSB), approximately 1,000 people are killed in train-related accidents each year.
Candice Bond, an experienced train accident attorney, said that in cases such as this that it is imperative to have an independent investigation conducted by a seasoned lawyer as soon as possible to preserve evidence for a victim's family.
“A skilled attorney can protect a grieving family from powerful train companies, which often send their investigators to crash sites and possess a variety of resources they can utilize to defend a negligent party,” said Bond, Managing Partner of Bond Legal and national analyst for The Legal Advocate.
WRONGFUL DEATH CLAIM
Pending results of the investigation, the family of Jamaal Scott may be entitled to a wrongful death claim. Family members of wrongful death victims may seek damages for loss of love and companionship, pain, suffering and emotional distress following a catastrophic accident such as this incident.
“The stress and sorrow can be extremely overwhelming after losing a loved one in an accident,” said Stefano Formica, Of Counsel with Bond Legal. “We understand just what families are forced to go through, and having their rights protected is vital. A wrongful death lawyer will stay updated on the official investigation and help ensure that their legal rights and best interests are protected.”

column4: Pregnant woman killed in crash involving Metrolink train and car in Glendale - ABC7 Los Angeles
column7:
Pregnant woman killed in crash involving Metrolink train and car in Glendale

GLENDALE, Calif. (KABC) -- A pregnant woman was killed Wednesday morning in a violent collision involving a Metrolink train and a car at a crossing in Glendale.

The crash was reported shortly after 9 a.m. on Brazil Street, just west of the intersection of San Fernando Road and Broadway, between the Burbank Downtown and the Glendale stations, according to Metrolink.

The victim was pronounced dead at the scene.

According to the Glendale Police Department, the sedan was traveling northbound on West San Fernando Road when the driver attempted to make a right turn onto Broadway, which turns into Brazil Street at that point.

"As the vehicle entered the crossing, it was struck by a Metrolink train traveling on the tracks," police said in a statement.

The downtown L.A.-bound train, number 206 on the Antelope Valley Line, was stopped on the tracks for hours after the collision.

A male pedestrian who was in the immediate area was struck by an object as a result of debris from the collision, police said. He was transported to a local hospital with non-life-threatening injuries.

"There are approximately 150 passengers on train 206, though no injuries have been reported aboard at this time," Metrolink said in a statement issued shortly after 10 a.m.

Trains 207 and 208 on the Antelope Valley Line were canceled, as were trains 320 and 341 on the San Bernardino Line. Metro buses were used to shuttle passengers between the Burbank Downtown and Glendale stations.

The Glendale Police Department was leading the investigation.

City News Service contributed to this report.
column114: A pregnant woman died after her sedan was struck by a Metrolink train in Glendale when she attempted to make a right turn at a crossing. The violent collision injured a male pedestrian with debris, stopped the train for hours, and caused significant service disruptions for other Metrolink lines.

column4: Woman killed, 4 injured after Metrolink train collides with car in Glendale - FOX 11 Los Angeles
column7:
Woman killed, 4 injured after Metrolink train collides with car in Glendale
GLENDALE, Calif. - Authorities are investigating a fatal crash involving a Metrolink train in Glendale that left a woman dead and several others injured on Wednesday.
What we know:
The incident was reported at approximately 9 a.m. on Brazil Street, situated just west of the intersection of San Fernando Road and Broadway.
According to the Glendale Police Department, the vehicle was traveling northbound on West San Fernando Road when the driver attempted to make a right turn onto Broadway, which turns into Brazil Street.
Once the car entered the crossing, it was struck by an oncoming Metrolink train, police said.
The victim, later identified by the Los Angeles County Medical Examiner as 35-year-old Zukhra Kurbanova, died at the scene.
Officials said around 150 passengers were on board at the time. Three passengers were injured, with one requiring hospitalization, authorities said.
A male bystander was also hospitalized after being struck by flying debris from the impact.
The incident led to the immediate cancellation of Trains 207 and 208 on the Antelope Valley Line, as well as Trains 320 and 341 on the San Bernardino Line.
To help stranded commuters, Metro buses were deployed to provide shuttle service between the Burbank Downtown and Glendale stations.
What's next:
The Glendale Police Department is leading the ongoing investigation into the crash.
The Source: This report is based on information from the Los Angeles Fire Department and the Glendale Police Department. City News Service contributed.

column4: Metrolink train slams into car in Glendale, killing 1 person, hospitalizing 2 - Los Angeles Times
column7: A Metrolink train traveling from the Antelope Valley to downtown Los Angeles slammed into a car Wednesday morning, killing one occupant of the car, according to authorities. Around 9 a.m., Metrolink Train 206 struck an occupied car between the Burbank and Glendale stations, according to Metrolink spokesperson Laurene Lopez. The crash occurred near San Fernando Road and West Broadway in Glendale. One occupant of the car died while another passenger and a bystander were hospitalized, Lopez said. Two passengers on the train were assessed and treated by the Glendale Fire Department and the Los Angeles County Fire Department. Coroner’s officials arrived around 11:50 a.m. and the tracks were still closed around noon, according to Lopez. The train was released to go into Glendale with passengers on board. The Glendale Police Department is the lead agency investigating the incident.Such collisions are rare but not unheard of.In June of last year, a semitruck collided with a Metrolink train in an agricultural area of Moorpark, killing the truck driver and sending one of the train passengers to the hospital, authorities said.In January 2025, a Metrolink train struck a car in the City of Industry, killing two people inside the vehicle, according to law enforcement officials.

column4: Woman Killed, 4 Others Injured When Train Crashes into Car That Was Crossing Tracks - People.com
column7:
A pregnant woman was killed, and four others were injured after a train slammed into a car that was crossing the tracks in California.

According to police in Glendale, Calif., a city bordering Los Angeles, the crash involved a Metrolink train that was traveling from the Antelope Valley to downtown Los Angeles at around 9 a.m. on Wednesday, Feb. 4.

Police said that a driver attempted to make a turn from the city's West San Fernando Road onto Broadway, crossing the railroad tracks.

As the car entered the railroad crossing, it was hit by Metrolink train 206, police wrote in a statement shared to Facebook.

Police and fire personnel were called to the scene, and the driver, identified as 35-year-old Zukhra Kurbanova, by authorities and FOX Los Angeles, was pronounced dead at the scene.

ABC7 and KTLA reported after the crash that Kurbanova was pregnant.

Additionally, police said that a man walking nearby was struck by debris. His injuries were not life-threatening, and he was taken to a local hospital.

Metrolink told the outlets that three passengers aboard the train also reported injuries, including one person who was hospitalized and two who were treated by emergency personnel on the train.

Footage from the local news outlets showed fire and police officials at the scene as the car, a silver sedan, sat on the tracks as the railroad's safety arms were down. The vehicle was heavily damaged, and its airbags were deployed.



"There are approximately 150 passengers on train 206, though no injuries have been reported aboard at this time," the rail operator told ABC7 in a statement.

The Los Angeles County Medical Examiner's Office told PEOPLE that the decedent was a resident of Glendale, and an exam has been scheduled.



When reached for comment, the Glendale Police Department said they could not release the identity of the deceased until the coroner's office's part of the investigation was complete.

"Additionally, this complex investigation will require some more time and our traffic investigator’s opinion as to why will be deferred until then," they added. "We do have knowledge that the arms at the tracks were in fact operational and operating at the time the driver made the right turn."



Never miss a story — sign up for PEOPLE's free daily newsletter to stay up-to-date on the best of what PEOPLE has to offer​​, from celebrity news to compelling human interest stories.



Trains 207 and 208 on the Antelope Valley Line were canceled for the day, Metrolink added, in addition to trains 320 and 341 on the San Bernardino Line. Instead of normal functions, buses were used to shuttle passengers to other stations.

column4: After Stockton derailment, California lawmaker pushes for tougher rail safety rules - CBS News
column7:
After Stockton derailment, California lawmaker pushes for tougher rail safety rules
A California congressman is calling for more railroad oversight. It comes on the three-year anniversary of one of the nation's most destructive railway accidents and just a day after a derailment in Stockton.
Most of the 17,000 Pacific Gas and Electric customers now have power restored following Monday's train accident in the Stockton railyards. But that mishap highlights the impact a train derailment can have on nearby communities.
"Millions of Americans live and work along hazmat railways," said Ed Kelly, general president of the International Association of Firefighters. "We need to protect them."
This week marks the three-year anniversary of the fire and explosion in East Palestine, Ohio, that was caused by a train derailment.
Congressman John Garamendi says similar tanker cars carrying flammable chemicals regularly come through Davis, Sacramento, and other Northern California communities.
"And we've had our share of almost-serious accidents," Garamendi said.
He is co-sponsoring a bipartisan bill that would require new railroad safety standards.
"This rail safety bill covers sensible things," Garamendi said. "It's not complex, but it is very, very important."
It would mandate having at least two crew members on board trains. It also requires railroads to install defect detectors that can spot mechanical problems, and it would force railroads to fund more hazmat training for first responders.
"Too often, firefighters arrive at train derailments with limited information and limited training," Kelly said.
Penalties for safety violations would also be increased.
The Association of American Railroads says there's no proven safety benefit to justify the cost of requiring two crew members. They oppose these kinds of regulations and want to adopt modern technologies to enhance safety benefits.
But bill supporters say more work needs to be done to protect communities from any more railway disasters.
The authors of the bill are trying to get it included in a vote for the new federal surface transportation reauthorization act.

column4: Community urges council to close train crossing after deaths - Palo Alto Daily Post
column7:
Palo Alto community members are urging City Council to close the intersection where Churchill Avenue intersects with the railroad tracks after a Caltrain hit and killed 17-year-old Summer Devi Mehta last Tuesday.

In a letter to the City Council Nick Mehta, Summer’s father, said he supports closing the intersection, believing Churchill provided an “easy means for her to impulsively act at a low moment.”

The letter was read by a friend of Nick Mehta’s during tonight’s council meeting.

The Caltrain crossing at Churchill Avenue and Alma Street is adjacent to Palo Alto High School, and has been the site of multiple student suicides over the past few years.

“It is too late for my daughter but it is not too late for others,” Nick Mehta wrote.

In response, Mayor Vicki Veenker said tonight that an The Rail Safety/Youth Mental Health ad hoc committee has been created to look into ramping up safety measures at the intersection, and the logistics of shutting it down, an action that Palo Alto Unified School District Superintendent Don Austin urged the council to do in a Thursday letter.

The committee consists of council members Julie Lythcott-Haims, Pat Burt and Greer Stone.

Lythcott-Haims said the committee has already met with Caltrain and other city transportation safety officials to explore their options, which included talks about installing more high-tech cameras that can detect unusual movements on the tracks and paying security guards to monitor the intersection. The committee will meet again this week, Lythcott-Haims said.

But several residents said they also want council to shut the Churchill crossing immediately, and not wait for the committee’s insights and recommendation.

A resident who identified herself as Bre. T said her daughter was Summer’s friend and is “begging” the council to close the intersection.

“Failing to close is a complacency I just can’t tolerate as a mom anymore,” she said.

Prior to the ad hoc meeting, Caltrain and Palo Alto were working on installing new technologies at Churchill crossings to improve safety, including using AI to help detect and alert officials about lingering cars and pedestrians on the tracks. Other potential updates include a partial underpass to reduce car and pedestrian traffic and grade separation, which would require temporarily closing the crossing.

column4: Santa Barbara County, CA – Train Accident Injures Man Near Summerland - localaccidentreports.com
column7:
Santa Barbara County, CA (February 12, 2026) – A man was seriously injured after being struck by a freight train between Lookout Park and Fernald Point near Summerland on Tuesday, February 10 just after 5 p.m., according to authorities.

Key Details

Reported: Tuesday, February 10 just after 5 p.m.

Tuesday, February 10 just after 5 p.m. Location: Railroad tracks between Lookout Park and Fernald Point, near Summerland

Railroad tracks between Lookout Park and Fernald Point, near Summerland Type of incident: Freight train versus pedestrian

Freight train versus pedestrian Injuries: Victim sustained serious injuries

Victim sustained serious injuries Medical response: Transported by AMR ambulance to Santa Barbara Cottage Hospital

Transported by AMR ambulance to Santa Barbara Cottage Hospital Responding agency: Montecito Fire Protection District and other emergency personnel

What We Know So Far

Firefighters located the injured pedestrian on the railroad tracks upon arrival.

Preliminary reports indicate a freight train struck the man under circumstances that remain under investigation.

The victim received first aid before being moved to Lookout Park and then transported to the hospital.

Rail service in the area was temporarily shut down and reopened around 7 p.m.

What’s Not Yet Confirmed

How the pedestrian came to be on the tracks at the time of the collision.

The identity of the injured man.

Whether any warning systems or signals were activated prior to the incident.

The man’s current medical condition.

Local Context

The incident occurred along the coastal railroad corridor between Lookout Park and Fernald Point near Summerland in Santa Barbara County. This stretch of track runs close to shoreline communities and recreational areas, including Lookout Park, and is used regularly by freight and passenger trains traveling through the region. When a train-related injury is reported in this area, responders from agencies such as the Montecito Fire Protection District must coordinate access along the tracks, which can be more difficult than roadway incidents. Temporary rail shutdowns may also affect service schedules while authorities secure the scene and conduct their investigation.

What to Do Next

If you witness a rail-related emergency, call 911 immediately and provide the exact location, including nearby landmarks such as Lookout Park or Fernald Point. Remain at a safe distance from railroad tracks and avoid attempting to approach or assist unless directed by emergency personnel. Write down what you observed, including train direction and timing, while the details are still fresh. Check official transit or rail advisories for updates if you plan to travel through the affected corridor.

FAQ

Q: Why are details sometimes limited right after a train accident?

A: Early information is often preliminary while investigators review scene evidence, speak with train operators and witnesses, and confirm the sequence of events.

Q: Are train-pedestrian crashes typically severe?

A: Collisions involving trains often result in serious injuries due to the size and speed of rail equipment, which can limit stopping distance.

Q: Can railroad operations be suspended after an incident?

A: Yes. Rail traffic may be temporarily halted so emergency crews can safely assist victims and investigators can examine the scene before service resumes.

column4: Person Taken to Hospital After Being Hit by Train Near Santa Barbara Cemetery - Noozhawk
column7:
A man died Friday night after being struck by a train near the Santa Barbara Cemetery, according to the Santa Barbara Police Department.
Personnel from the Santa Barbara City Fire Department and the Montecito Fire Protection District responded shortly after 5:45 p.m. to the report of a train-versus-pedestrian collision near the 1800 block of East Cabrillo Boulevard, fire Battalion Chief Jon Turner told Noozhawk.
Police officers also responded and started CPR, which was taken over by fire crews, Turner said.
The victim, who was found on the south side of the tracks, was transported by AMR ambulance to Santa Barbara Cottage Hospital, where he succumbed to his injuries, police said.
His name was not released pending notification of relatives.
Responders confirmed the victim was hit by a northbound Amtrak passenger train, Turner added.
The incident was under investigation and no other information was immediately available.
This is the third incident of a person being hit and injured by a train in southern Santa Barbara County this month.
A man was struck and killed by a northbound Amtrak Pacific Surfliner train on Feb. 1 in Santa Barbara near Milpas Street. The Coroner’s Bureau is investigating and has not yet released his name.
On Tuesday, a man was transported to the hospital with major injuries after being hit by a freight train in Summerland. The man was hit on a trestle on the tracks between Lookout Park and Fernald Point.

column4: Early-Morning Train Collision Involves Stuck Vehicle - The E’ville Eye
column7:
A vehicle was struck by a train early Saturday morning in Emeryville after a driver accidentally turned onto the railroad tracks near 67th and Shellmound and became stuck, police said.

The collision occurred around 1:09 a.m. on February 14, prompting a response from emergency personnel. According to the Emeryville Police Department, the female driver was able to safely exit the vehicle before an oncoming train struck it. The vehicle caught fire following the impact.

No injuries were reported, and authorities said all involved parties remained on scene and cooperated with the investigation. Police noted there were no signs of impairment and DUI was not suspected.

Rail service in the area was temporarily shut down for several hours while crews worked to extinguish the fire, remove the vehicle, and conduct a full inspection of the tracks. The vehicle was cleared without further incident, and officials emphasized there was no threat to the public.

Emeryville police thanked Union Pacific Railroad and the Alameda County Fire Department for their coordinated response.

Authorities also reminded residents that if a vehicle becomes stuck on train tracks, drivers should call 911 immediately so emergency personnel can halt rail traffic and respond quickly.

EMERYVILLE, Calif. - A tanker train crashed into a car that was stopped on the tracks in Emeryville early on Saturday morning, causing a small fire and damage to the rails.

The Alameda County Fire Department told KTVU firefighters were called about 1:10 a.m. to the tracks intersecting Shellmound Street, near 67th Street, on reports of the crash.

Firefighters found the car burning on the tracks and quickly doused the flames.

The Emeryville Police Department told KTVU that a woman accidentally turned her car onto the train tracks, where the vehicle became stuck. She stepped out of her car before it was struck by the oncoming train.

No injuries were reported in the crash, though the fire department said the crash and fire caused damage to the tracks. The road in the area was also shut down for several hours to allow crews to remove the wrecked car from the scene.

Police said DUI was not suspected to be a factor in the crash.

Pedestrian Killed After Being Struck by Train Near Chico State Campus

A pedestrian killed after being struck by train near Chico State early Monday morning, February 9, 2026, according to the Chico Police Department. The fatal collision occurred at approximately 1:51 a.m. near West Sacramento Avenue and North Cedar Street, close to the Chico State campus and nearby student housing.

What Happened

Police officers responding to the scene found an adult male deceased on the railroad tracks. Authorities reported that the engineer of a northbound Amtrak train saw a person on the tracks but was unable to stop the train in time to avoid the collision.

Witness interviews and preliminary findings indicate there were no suspicious circumstances leading up to the incident, though the investigation remains ongoing. The train remained stopped at the scene for approximately two hours, while vehicle traffic in the surrounding area was not impacted.

Chico police stated that the man is not believed to be a Chico State student. The Butte County Sheriff’s Office has taken custody of the remains, and the victim’s identity is being withheld pending notification of next of kin.

Investigation Ongoing

Authorities have a preliminary identification of the deceased but will not release the name until family members are notified. Law enforcement continues to review the circumstances surrounding the incident.

Railroad & Pedestrian Safety

Railroad tracks are extremely dangerous areas, particularly at night when visibility is limited. Trains require long distances to stop and cannot maneuver to avoid pedestrians. Individuals are urged to stay clear of tracks at all times, obey posted warnings, and avoid walking near rail lines, especially in low-light conditions.

column4: Metrolink train and semi collide in Moorpark, killing truck's driver - AOL.com
column7:
Metrolink train and semi collide in Moorpark, killing truck's driver
A semitruck collided with a Metrolink train in an agricultural area of Moorpark on Wednesday afternoon, killing the truck driver and sending one of the train passengers to the hospital, authorities said.
Metrolink train 118 struck a truck and trailer at a crossing on private farmland near the intersection of Los Angeles Avenue and Montair Drive at 2:15 p.m., according to a Metrolink spokesperson. The truck driver was pronounced dead at the scene, and one of the train's 11 passengers was transported to a local hospital for treatment in unknown condition, the spokesperson said.
The truck was severely damaged in the collision. A section of the vehicle was pushed one-third of a mile down the track, said Fire Department spokesperson Andy VanSciver. All five train cars and the engine remained upright on the tracks following the collision.
Advertisement
The cause of the accident is currently unknown. Train crossings on agricultural land in Ventura County are typically marked with stop signs and signs directing drivers to look both ways, said VanSciver.
As a result of the collision, Metrolink service that regularly operates to Ventura East Station was only operating as far as Moorpark. Metrolink on Wednesday afternoon was working to provide alternative transportation and Uber vouchers to affected customers, who can call their customer hotline at (800) 371-5465 for assistance.
This story originally appeared in Los Angeles Times.

Authorities reported that the driver, a 58-year-old woman, was outside of her vehicle at the time of the incident. She sustained fatal injuries and was pronounced dead at the scene. Her identity is being withheld pending notification of next of kin.
Investigators are working to determine the circumstances that led to the vehicle being on the tracks. The incident remains under active investigation.
Safety Tips Around Railroad Crossings
Railroad incidents are often catastrophic due to the size and stopping distance of trains. Drivers can help reduce the risk by:
- Never stopping or parking on railroad tracks
- Obeying all crossing signals, gates, and warning lights
- Looking both ways before crossing, even if signals are not active
- Avoiding distractions and staying alert near rail crossings
- Exiting the vehicle immediately and moving away from the tracks if a car becomes stuck
Trains require a long distance to come to a complete stop, making early caution essential.

column4: Wrongful Death Attorneys Navigate Complex California Tragedies - Azat TV
column7:
SACRAMENTO (Azat TV) – Recent, tragic incidents across California, including a deadly avalanche in the Sierra Nevada and a fatal train collision near Wheatland, have thrust the critical role of wrongful death attorneys into the spotlight. These cases highlight the complex legal landscape families face when seeking accountability and compensation after unexpected fatalities, especially when questions of negligence or liability arise.

On February 17, 2026, a 58-year-old woman was killed in a train collision near State Route 65 and Dairy Road in Wheatland, Yuba County, when a freight train struck her Toyota Prius. Authorities confirmed the driver was outside her vehicle at the time of impact. Just days later, on February 21, 2026, a massive avalanche in the remote Sierra Nevada wilderness claimed eight lives and left one presumed dead during a guided backcountry ski trip. Both incidents are under active investigation, raising profound questions about the circumstances leading to these deaths and the potential for legal recourse through wrongful death claims.

The California avalanche, which involved clients of Blackbird Mountain Guides, has sparked an unusual criminal negligence investigation by the Nevada County Sheriff’s Office, a move legal experts describe as having scant precedent. Typically, backcountry tour clients sign liability waivers that shield guide companies. However, according to Utah ski injury attorney Rob Miner, these waivers do not absolve guides of responsibility if they fail to follow appropriate safety protocols or adequately inform clients of risks. Miner emphasized, “They’re guides. It is presumed they will not guide you into an experience that may kill you, or that creates an unreasonable risk of death.”

The investigation is scrutinizing the guides’ decision to embark on the three-day trip with 11 clients amidst intensifying storm warnings and then to ski out on Tuesday as avalanche conditions worsened. This slide was the deadliest in the U.S. since 1981, making the scale and the decision-making process pivotal for investigators. Attorney Andrew McDevitt noted that while skiing is recognized as inherently dangerous, the specific circumstances of this incident are drawing a closer look. Ravn Whitington, an attorney specializing in ski injury and wrongful death cases near the avalanche site in Truckee, California, acknowledged the ongoing speculation, stating that much of what investigators know remains undisclosed to the public.

In the aftermath of the Wheatland train collision, the California Highway Patrol is actively investigating the factors that led to the vehicle being on the railroad tracks. Such incidents are often catastrophic, necessitating thorough investigations to determine all contributing factors, which can include roadway design, crossing conditions, visibility issues, or mechanical problems. In these complex scenarios, an experienced wrongful death attorney plays a crucial role in advocating for the victim’s family.

Wrongful death attorneys conduct independent investigations, working to preserve critical evidence and accident data. They often collaborate with reconstruction and safety experts to identify all potentially responsible parties, which can extend beyond the immediate incident to include third-party factors. Their expertise is vital in helping families pursue compensation for various damages, including funeral expenses, lost financial support, and the profound loss of companionship. Furthermore, these legal professionals manage communications with insurance companies and other involved entities, allowing grieving families to focus on healing without the added burden of legal complexities.

Both the avalanche and train collision cases underscore the specialized knowledge required to navigate wrongful death claims. In California, courts recognize skiing as an inherently dangerous activity, meaning recreational skiers assume certain risks. However, the unique circumstances of the guided avalanche trip, particularly the alleged disregard for severe weather warnings, could form the basis for successful claims of criminal negligence or civil liability against the guide company. Similarly, in train collisions, determining liability often involves extensive technical and legal analysis to identify all contributing factors and potentially responsible parties.

The confluence of these high-profile tragedies in California highlights the increasing demand for specialized legal counsel capable of dissecting complex accident scenarios, challenging liability waivers, and ensuring that families receive comprehensive support and justice in the face of profound loss.

column4: 8 injured at Union Station as Metrolink train slams into stationary train - AOL.com
column7:
Eight people were injured Wednesday morning when a Metrolink train arriving at Union Station crashed into a stationary train, authorities said.

The incident took place around 7:30 a.m., and one passenger was taken to a hospital for treatment, said Metrolink spokesperson Brett Hawkins. Six passengers and the train conductor suffered minor injuries.

Ventura County Line Train 104 was carrying 85 passengers when it slammed into an empty Metrolink train at the platform. The cause of the crash is under investigation by Metrolink's safety, security and compliance department.

The impacted trains were going to be used for San Bernardino Line Trains 310 and 327, both of which were then canceled.

"Fortunately, at that time of morning, we have San Bernardino Line trains running every 30 minutes, so the operational impact wasn't too heavy," said Hawkins.

In June, a semitruck collided with a Metrolink train in Moorpark, killing the truck driver and sending one passenger to the hospital. In January, a Metrolink train struck a car in the City of Industry, killing two people inside the vehicle.

column7:
South Los Angeles, California – At least nine people were injured Thursday evening after a collision between a Metro train and an SUV in the Central-Alameda area of South Los Angeles, authorities confirmed.

The crash occurred around 5:40 p.m. near 5427 S. Long Beach Ave., prompting a rapid response from the Los Angeles Fire Department. Firefighters arriving at the scene began working to extricate a 48-year-old male driver who was trapped inside the SUV following the impact.

In total, nine individuals were evaluated by first responders.

Four people — including the SUV’s driver — were transported to a nearby hospital with what officials described as minor injuries. The remaining five individuals declined medical transport after being assessed at the scene.

Authorities said the train operator and one female patient were also evaluated for minor complaints. Fire crews conducted a thorough inspection of the train to confirm there were no additional injuries among passengers.

The collision caused significant disruption in the area, and officials warned that traffic delays are expected to continue for an extended period. Motorists were advised to avoid the intersection and seek alternate routes while emergency crews worked to clear the scene and investigators began reviewing the circumstances of the crash.

The cause of the collision remains under investigation. Officials have not yet released details regarding what led to the train and SUV occupying the same crossing at the time of the crash.

Anyone who witnessed the incident or has information that may assist investigators is encouraged to contact local authorities.

column4: Missed Turn Causes Train-Truck Collision - Heavy Duty Trucking
column7:
Seven people were hurt and a $1.5 million oversize load was destroyed last week near Los Angeles when a truck driver missed his turn and bottomed out on railroad tracks, where the rig was hit by a commuter train.
The crash happened early Friday morning in Glendale, CA. The truck, operated by Mercury Transportation of Houston and escorted by California Highway Patrol vehicles, continued straight on over the tracks instead of making the left turn it was supposed to. CHP officers alerted the caravan to the mistake and told the trucker to retrace his steps. When he crossed back over the tracks, the 110,000-pound, 19-foot-high carbon dioxide condensing unit got stuck. The warning gates came down, and less than a minute later, the Metrolink train crashed into the truck. The driver, who had gotten out of the cab to try to raise the trailer, was not injured. Seven people on the train received minor injuries.
In a preliminary investigation, officials said that the ultimate responsibility for the accident lies with the driver, and not with the pilot vehicle or the CHP officers contracted to escort them.
Although the CHP was hired to escort the truck, its responsibility was to make sure that other motorists are not hurt or damaged by the truck, according to the patrol. It's the pilot vehicle and the truck driver's responsibility to get there safely, a CHP spokesman told the Los Angeles Times.
Officials are also investigating the possible lack of an oversized permit for the load to travel through Glendale.

column4: San Bernardino pursuit suspect crashes into train while fleeing from police - CBS News
column7:
San Bernardino pursuit suspect crashes into train while fleeing from police
San Bernardino police officers were in pursuit of a stolen vehicle on Wednesday afternoon when the suspect drove onto some train tracks and collided with a train.
Officers began the chase after learning of a car that had been stolen out of Colton. It was detected by a San Bernardino Police Department helicopter that was flying overhead, thanks to the vehicle's LoJack GPS detection system.
When SBPD officers tried to pull the driver over, the driver fled, prompting the chase to begin. As the chase continued, the suspect turned onto the train tracks near Mount Vernon Avenue and continued driving until they collided with a train, police said.
After the collision, the suspect allegedly got out of the car and ran until they climbed on top of a pipe in the area, where they temporarily resisted arrest before being taken into custody, according to SBPD.
Aerial footage shows the truck just feet away from a railing over the Santa Ana River.
No further information was provided.

When SBPD officers tried to pull the driver over, the driver fled, prompting the chase to begin. As the chase continued, the suspect turned onto the train tracks near Mount Vernon Avenue and continued driving until they collided with a train, police said.
After the collision, the suspect allegedly got out of the car and ran until they climbed on top of a pipe in the area, where they temporarily resisted arrest before being taken into custody, according to SBPD.
Aerial footage shows the truck just feet away from a railing over the Santa Ana River.
No further information was provided.

column4: Los Angeles, CA – Pedestrian Killed in Train Accident near Media Center Drive in Glassell ParkAccording to the Los Angeles Fire Department, the incident was reported at around 11:30 a.m. March 11, in the area of 2705 Media Center Drive west of San Ferna - facebook.com

Los Angeles, CA – Pedestrian Killed in Train Accident near Media Center Drive in Glassell ParkAccording to the Los Angeles Fire Department, the incident was reported at around 11:30 a.m. March 11, in the area of 2705 Media Center Drive west of San Fernando Road

Los Angeles, CA – Authorities responded to an accident in which a pedestrian was hit and killed by a train in the Glassell Park neighborhood of Los Angeles on Wednesday morning.

According to the Los Angeles Fire Department, the incident was reported at around 11:30 a.m. March 11, in the area of 2705 Media Center Drive west of San Fernando Road.

Initial reports indicate that an Amtrak train hit a person on the tracks. It’s unclear why the victim was on the tracks when they were hit.

The Los Angeles Fire Department crews and Los Angeles Police Department officers arrived at the scene shortly after.

The medics confirmed the pedestrian’s death at the site of the collision. The victim’s name, age, and gender were not immediately released.

No one on the train reported any injuries as a result of the collision.

At this time, no additional details on the incident have been released. Investigations are ongoing to establish how and why the accident occurred.

Pedestrians remain the most vulnerable group, accounting for over 50% of all fatalities (150+ deaths in 2025), while severe injuries citywide have remained high, consistently exceeding 1,500 annually since 2022.

column4: Deadly Train Accident Near Glassell Park Warehouse - Hoodline
column7:
A pedestrian was struck and killed by a train late Wednesday morning in Glassell Park, in a deadly collision that unfolded near a warehouse-lined stretch of tracks. The crash was reported around 11:30 a.m. near 2705 Media Center Drive, where emergency crews pronounced the victim dead at the scene. Responders reported no other patients connected to the incident.

Scene and response

According to the Los Angeles Fire Department, crews from Fire Station 50, along with multiple battalion, engine, and rescue units, responded to the rail emergency and logged the call as Inc#0734. Fire officials reported no additional patients on or around the train and said the Los Angeles Police Department and the Los Angeles County Medical Examiner would handle body recovery under LAPD Inc#2132.

Where it happened

The crash took place in the Media Center industrial corridor of northeast Los Angeles, near a warehouse property at 2705 Media Center Drive. Public records list the address as a large one-story warehouse and distribution site, and the location underscores how rail right-of-way cuts through mixed industrial and residential stretches in the neighborhood. PropertyShark confirms the site details.

Investigation and context

Pedestrian safety continues to pose problems across Los Angeles, and the LAPD has highlighted targeted enforcement and public outreach as part of ongoing efforts to curb traffic deaths. Train-involved collisions in the region have also led to lengthy investigations and service disruptions in the past, as reported by the Los Angeles Times, a reminder of how a single trackside incident can ripple through the broader transportation system.

What's next

LAPD traffic investigators are handling the on-scene probe, while the County medical examiner oversees body recovery and will determine the cause and manner of death. Authorities have not released any identifying information about the victim, and the investigation remains active as agencies say they will share additional details when they are available.

column4: Family of Atwater man found dead near railroad tracks raising money for funeral - Merced Sun-Star
column7:
The mother of an Atwater man found dead near a railroad crossing on Sunday night has started a GoFundMe fundraiser to help pay for his funeral expenses.

The family is looking to raise money to give Lucas Alexander Raphel Benavidez, 26, a proper funeral.

As of the morning of Friday, March 13, $1,506 raised of the goal of $2K has been raised.

Atwater police closed the railroad crossings on Atwater Boulevard at Applegate Road and on Packers and High Street at about 6 p.m. Sunday to investigate, the Atwater Police Department said on Facebook. The crossings were open a few hours later.

“He was taken from us far too soon in a tragic accident involving a train, leaving a void in our family, our hearts, our lives and those of countless others that can never be filled,” Heather Burns said in the GoFundMe post.

This story was originally published March 11, 2026 at 8:12 AM.

column4: Train kills person in San Mateo - Palo Alto Daily Post
column7:
Caltrain photo.

A Caltrain hit and killed a person on Saturday in San Mateo, authorities confirmed today (March 16).
The person who died has not been publicly identified by the San Mateo County Coroner’s Office, pending notification of their next of kin.
The crash occurred near E. Bellevue Ave. around 5:30 p.m.
Caltrain has a policy of no longer telling the public about deaths on the tracks in an attempt to prevent copycat suicides. It is not known whether Saturday’s death was an accident or suicide.
Caltrain reported 10 deaths in 2022, 15 deaths in 2023 and 19 deaths in 2024 — the most in a decade.
Another 10 people died last year, according to a fatality log provided by the agency.
